parameters.content:
## Configure your email SMTP Settings here

1) Basic Settings

From Email: Your email address (must match your SMTP credentials in most cases).

To: Use a variable, e.g. ={{$json.to}} from your Function node.

Subject: ={{$json.subject}}

HTML Content: ={{$json.htmlBody}}

Text Content (optional fallback): ={{$json.textBody}}

2) SMTP Credentials

When you edit the node, you’ll see Credentials → SMTP. Add new credentials and fill:

Host: (depends on provider)

Gmail: smtp.gmail.com

Outlook/Office365: smtp.office365.com

AWS SES: email-smtp.<region>.amazonaws.com

Port:

465 (SSL) or 587 (TLS/STARTTLS)

User: Your full email address or SMTP username.

Password: Your email app password or SMTP password.

3) Special Notes per Provider

Gmail:

Must enable App Passwords (requires 2FA).

Use smtp.gmail.com, port 465 (SSL) or 587 (TLS).

Outlook/Office365:

Use smtp.office365.com, port 587.

Username = full email, password = normal login password (or app password if enforced).

AWS SES:

Region-specific host (e.g., email-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com).

Create SMTP user in SES console → copy credentials.
Configure a Cron Node (Scheduler Trigger) here or You may trigger this node Manually when you need to trigger sending an email

Set variables to 
send with email here
Set email body and subject with the pre-configured variables from the previous node
Send email with your email host
